WebSocket - Tools - Comkit: ComKit is a Swift library that provides a simplified way to build WebSocket clients and servers. It abstracts away the complexities of working with SwiftNIO, making it easier to develop WebSocket-based applications in Swift.
WSS: WSS refers to WebSocket Secure, a protocol for establishing a secure, real-time, two-way communication channel between a web client and a server using TLS/SSL encryption. It's similar to HTTP and HTTPS, but for WebSocket connections, with 'ws' being the unencrypted version and 'wss' being the encrypted one.
Streaming - Output - Video - Progressive: Progressive streaming downloads a single video file, playing it as it loads, which can cause buffering on slow connections.
Streaming - Output - Video - Adaptive: Adaptive streaming (ABR) breaks video into segments with multiple quality levels, switching in real-time based on bandwidth to prevent buffering and optimize quality.
Streaming - Output - Video - Adaptive -: HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) is a widely used, Apple-developed adaptive bitrate protocol that delivers high-quality live and on-demand video over standard HTTP.

a widely used, Apple-developed adaptive bitrate protocol that delivers high-quality live and on-demand video over standard HTTP. It breaks video into small .ts segments, using .m3u8 playlists to manage streaming, allowing players to adjust quality instantly based on network conditions.
MPEG-DASH (Dynamic Adaptive Streaming over HTTP) is an international standard for HTTP-based adaptive streaming that enables high-quality video delivery by adjusting quality to network conditions.
It breaks video into segments (fMP4/WebM) and uses an XML-based Media Presentation Description (MPD) manifest file to manage varying bitrates.
